Use this letter as evidence if any incorrect debit or claim arises.

cat_wohnen_mietvertrag_summary: This document is the complete legal foundation of the relationship between you and the landlord. It defines the start of the tenancy, the rent amount, additional costs, the contract duration, and the security deposit, as well as conditions for use and termination. It is the primary reference for any future dispute about the tenancy, payments, or duration.
cat_wohnen_mietvertrag_step1: Check your name, the landlord's name, and the apartment address character by character.
cat_wohnen_mietvertrag_step2: Extract the Kaltmiete, Betriebskosten, and Gesamtmiete as separate figures.
cat_wohnen_mietvertrag_step3: Check whether the contract is unbefristet (open-ended) or befristet (fixed-term).
cat_wohnen_mietvertrag_step4: Review the Kaution clause, the payment method, and any annexes.
cat_wohnen_mietvertrag_step5: Read the Hausordnung, subletting, and pet clauses, and any special obligations.
cat_wohnen_mietvertrag_step6: Save a clear PDF copy together with all Anlagen linked to the contract.
cat_wohnen_wohnungsgeberbestaetigung_sum: This document is not a rental contract; it is a legal confirmation required specifically for registering your address at the Bürgeramt or Meldebehörde. It proves who the Wohnungsgeber is, what the address is, who moved in, and on what date. A Mietvertrag does not replace it for Anmeldung purposes.
cat_wohnen_wohnungsgeberbestaetigung_ste: Confirm that the name, address, and move-in date exactly match reality.
Check that the Wohnungsgeber's details are complete.
If the Wohnungsgeber is not the owner, make sure the Eigentümer's details are also included.
Use it only for official registration — do not mix it up with the rental contract.
Save a very clear copy of it.
If more than one person is moving in, ensure all required names are present.
cat_wohnen_mietbescheinigung_summary: This document is not a contract but a tenancy and rent data certificate, usually filled in for submission to an official body. It focuses on core housing data such as the address, size, tenancy start date, base rent, and monthly costs, and is formally completed by the vermietende Person.
cat_wohnen_mietbescheinigung_step1: Verify the address, apartment size, and tenancy start date carefully.
cat_wohnen_mietbescheinigung_step2: Separate the Grundmiete from the Betriebskosten and Heizkosten.
cat_wohnen_mietbescheinigung_step3: Confirm that the landlord's signature and details are present.
cat_wohnen_mietbescheinigung_step4: Use it only with the authority that requested it.
cat_wohnen_mietbescheinigung_step5: Keep it together with the Mietvertrag in the same file.
cat_wohnen_mietbescheinigung_step6: If the data does not match the contract, correct it before submitting.
cat_wohnen_nebenkostenabrechnung_summary: This letter is the annual or periodic settlement of additional costs. What matters is not only the final figure but also the billing period, the amount of advance payments, the distribution method, and the tenant's share. Legally, the Abrechnung is done annually on advance payments and must include total costs, the distribution key, and the tenant's calculated share in a comprehensible way.
cat_wohnen_nebenkostenabrechnung_step2: Determine whether the result is a Nachzahlung (extra payment) or Guthaben (credit).
cat_wohnen_nebenkostenabrechnung_step3: Compare the Vorauszahlungen with what was actually charged.
cat_wohnen_nebenkostenabrechnung_step4: Review the Verteilerschlüssel and the calculation method.
cat_wohnen_nebenkostenabrechnung_step5: Do not pay immediately if the figures are unclear or illogical.
cat_wohnen_nebenkostenabrechnung_step6: Keep it in a separate file for annual housing costs.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_summary: This letter means the landlord is requesting a rent adjustment on a legal basis that must be justified. A Mieterhöhung to Vergleichsmiete requires Textform and a Begründung, and may rely on a Mietspiegel, Vergleichswohnungen, or an expert report. The increase generally takes effect at the start of the third calendar month after the request arrives, if approved or resolved legally.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_step1: Extract the current and new rent figures precisely.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_step2: Identify the actual effective start date.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_step3: Read the Begründung section in full.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_step4: Check whether the letter explicitly requests a Zustimmung.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_step5: Save the old and new amounts together in one file for comparison.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_step6: Do not ignore this letter — misunderstanding it can lead to a later dispute.
cat_wohnen_kautionsabrechnung_summary: This broad category covers deposit-related documents after the end of a tenancy. Practical documents divide clearly into: agreement, payment confirmation, detailed settlement, withholding, and refund. Use more specific subcategories where possible to avoid classification conflicts.
cat_wohnen_kautionsabrechnung_step1: Identify the specific type of deposit document (agreement, receipt, settlement, withholding, or refund).
cat_wohnen_kautionsabrechnung_step2: Extract the deposit amount and any deductions listed.
cat_wohnen_kautionsabrechnung_step3: Check whether the document relates to the start or end of the tenancy.
cat_wohnen_kautionsabrechnung_step4: Link this document with the Übergabeprotokoll and any prior correspondence.
cat_wohnen_kautionsabrechnung_step5: Keep it in the Kaution folder, separate from general housing costs.
cat_wohnen_kautionsabrechnung_step6: Do not treat it as closed if deductions are unexplained.
cat_wohnen_modernisierungsankuendigung_s: This letter is not a simple repair notice but a legal announcement of a Modernisierungsmaßnahme. The law requires written notice at least three months in advance, stating the type and scope of the work, its expected start date and duration, and any expected rent increase or change in utility costs, along with a reference to the right to raise a Härteeinwand.
Read the description of the work item by item.
Extract the expected start date and duration.
Check whether the work is inside your apartment or only in the building.
Review whether there is an expected future financial impact.
Note any deadline linked to raising a Härteeinwand.
Keep the letter with any later notice related to the same project.
cat_wohnen_reparaturankuendigung_summary: This letter means a specific appointment has been set for a technician, company, or inspection service to access the apartment. The focus is on the time and access to the apartment, not on any contract change. In practice, missing appointments with German housing providers may result in a second appointment, an estimate, or additional costs depending on the type of service.
cat_wohnen_reparaturankuendigung_step1: Extract the day, time, and type of work directly.
cat_wohnen_reparaturankuendigung_step2: Understand whether the appointment is inside your apartment or only in the building.
cat_wohnen_reparaturankuendigung_step3: Check whether your personal presence is required or if arranging access is enough.
cat_wohnen_reparaturankuendigung_step4: If you are unavailable, request a replacement appointment quickly.
cat_wohnen_reparaturankuendigung_step5: Keep the letter with any follow-up reminders.
cat_wohnen_reparaturankuendigung_step6: Do not confuse this with Modernisierung — it is shorter and narrower in scope.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_kuendigung_summ: This letter means the tenancy relationship is ending on a specific date. Legally, a Kündigung requires Schriftform, and for an ordentliche Kündigung there are specific notice periods under §573c BGB. In practice these documents clearly show either 'hiermit kündige ich' or 'Kündigung des Mietverhältnisses' or a specific termination date.
cat_wohnen_mieterhoehung_kuendigung_step: Extract the exact end date of the contract.
Understand whether this is a standard termination or one linked to a specific condition.
Begin arranging the Übergabe, meter readings, and key handover.
Keep a copy with proof of sending or receipt if the notice came from you.
Link it later with the Übergabeprotokoll and Kaution.
If it is missing a signature or date, do not treat it as final before verifying.
cat_wohnen_fristlose_kuendigung_summary: This is one of the most serious letters in a housing file. Its legal basis is the außerordentliche fristlose Kündigung wegen Zahlungsverzug under §543 and §569 BGB, usually based on a legally significant Mietrückstand or repeated delays. This is not an ordinary reminder — it is a direct escalation threatening the continuation of the tenancy itself.
cat_wohnen_fristlose_kuendigung_step1: Treat this letter as an extremely urgent matter.
cat_wohnen_fristlose_kuendigung_step2: Extract the amount of arrears and the period on which the termination is based.
cat_wohnen_fristlose_kuendigung_step3: Understand whether specific months or a cumulative total are referenced.
cat_wohnen_fristlose_kuendigung_step4: Gather all payment proofs and prior correspondence immediately.
cat_wohnen_fristlose_kuendigung_step5: Keep the letter in a high-risk section of the app.
cat_wohnen_fristlose_kuendigung_step6: Act quickly — legally or administratively — if necessary.
cat_wohnen_mieterrueckstand_summary: This letter means there is an outstanding or unpaid rent, but it is usually still at the reminder or Mahnung stage before reaching a fristlose Kündigung. The most important things to identify are: which month or period was not paid, how much is owed, and what the new deadline is.
cat_wohnen_mieterrueckstand_step1: Immediately check whether there is actually an unpaid rental amount.
cat_wohnen_mieterrueckstand_step2: Extract the outstanding amount and the relevant month or months.
cat_wohnen_mieterrueckstand_step3: Identify the latest payment deadline mentioned.
cat_wohnen_mieterrueckstand_step4: If you have already paid, link the proof of payment to this letter.
cat_wohnen_mieterrueckstand_step5: If you have not yet paid, treat this as a serious warning before escalation.
cat_wohnen_mieterrueckstand_step6: Keep it with any subsequent Mahnung in the correct chronological order.
cat_wohnen_hausordnung_summary: This is not a financial letter or a warning, but an internal rules document governing daily life in the building. Typical entries clearly include words such as Lärm, Rücksichtnahme, Treppenhaus, Sauberkeit, and Ruhezeiten. Note that 'dates' here are not deadlines but times of use.
cat_wohnen_hausordnung_step1: Read the Lärm and Ruhezeiten sections first.
cat_wohnen_hausordnung_step2: Understand what is permitted and what is prohibited in shared areas.
cat_wohnen_hausordnung_step3: Keep it with the contract, as some contracts make it part of your obligations.
cat_wohnen_hausordnung_step4: Do not treat its times as administrative deadlines within the app.
cat_wohnen_hausordnung_step5: If a new version exists, store the old and new versions together.
cat_wohnen_hausordnung_step6: Do not mix it with maintenance notices or financial demands.
cat_wohnen_uebergabeprotokoll_summary: This is one of the most important documents at move-in or move-out, as it records the actual condition of the unit at the moment of handover: defects, meter readings, and key count. In any later dispute about damage, deposit, or consumption, everyone typically turns to this document first.
cat_wohnen_uebergabeprotokoll_step1: Review every field visually, not just formally.
cat_wohnen_uebergabeprotokoll_step2: Confirm that Strom, Wasser, and Gas readings are recorded if applicable.
cat_wohnen_uebergabeprotokoll_step3: Check the number of keys received or handed over.
cat_wohnen_uebergabeprotokoll_step4: Take photos or a video on the same day as signing.
cat_wohnen_uebergabeprotokoll_step5: Do not sign 'in Ordnung' if there is a remark that was not written down.
cat_wohnen_uebergabeprotokoll_step6: Keep it directly alongside the Mietvertrag and Kaution.
cat_wohnen_kautionsvereinbarung_summary: This category concerns the agreement on the deposit itself, not the confirmation of payment nor the refund. Legally, the Mietsicherheit must be agreed in the Mietvertrag or a Nachtrag, and the tenant is entitled to pay it in three equal monthly instalments, with the first instalment due at the start of the Mietverhältnis.
cat_wohnen_kautionsvereinbarung_step1: Extract the Kautionsbetrag and the agreed payment method.
cat_wohnen_kautionsvereinbarung_step2: Verify that the amount matches the main rental contract.
cat_wohnen_kautionsvereinbarung_step3: Understand whether the agreement is inside the Mietvertrag or in a separate Nachtrag.
cat_wohnen_kautionsvereinbarung_step4: Extract any reference to a Kautionskonto, Sparbuch, or Bürgschaft.
cat_wohnen_kautionsvereinbarung_step5: Keep the document in the Kaution folder, not in the general housing costs folder.
cat_wohnen_kautionsvereinbarung_step6: Do not mix it with a receipt or a refund document.
cat_wohnen_kaution_bestaetigung_summary: This document is the practical proof that the deposit amount was actually paid or received. In cash or manual payment situations the tenant usually receives a Quittung. This is very important because later disputes often start with the question: was the deposit paid at all, and how much was it?
cat_wohnen_kaution_bestaetigung_step2: Check whether payment was made in full or in instalments.
cat_wohnen_kaution_bestaetigung_step3: Review the receipt date and the name of the signatory.
cat_wohnen_kaution_bestaetigung_step4: If payment was made via Sparbuch or another form, keep the details with it.
cat_wohnen_kaution_bestaetigung_step5: Link it directly with the Kautionsvereinbarung or Mietvertrag.
cat_wohnen_kaution_bestaetigung_step6: Keep it as an independent proof — the need for it usually arises later.
cat_wohnen_kaution_abrechnung_summary: This letter is the final or near-final settlement of the deposit after the end of the tenancy. It does not just say how much will be returned; it explains the deposit amount, any interest, and the offsetting deductions or claims. Part of the deposit may also be kept pending if a Nebenkostenabrechnung or other claims are still outstanding.
cat_wohnen_kaution_abrechnung_step1: Start with the original deposit amount.
cat_wohnen_kaution_abrechnung_step4: Check whether the reasons for deductions are explained or are just numbers.
cat_wohnen_kaution_abrechnung_step5: Compare with the Übergabeprotokoll and any prior claims.
cat_wohnen_kaution_abrechnung_step6: If deductions are unclear, do not consider the file closed.
cat_wohnen_kaution_einbehalt_summary: This category means the money will not be returned in full now because the landlord or management has decided to withhold part of the deposit temporarily or permanently. The difference from a Kautionsabrechnung is that the focus here is on the act of withholding and its reason, not on a completed final settlement. Practically, part of the deposit may be kept open for expected claims such as Nebenkosten or disputed damage.
cat_wohnen_kaution_einbehalt_step1: Read the reason for the Einbehalt carefully.
cat_wohnen_kaution_einbehalt_step2: Determine whether the withholding is partial or total.
cat_wohnen_kaution_einbehalt_step3: Link the letter immediately with the Übergabeprotokoll and any photos or invoices.
cat_wohnen_kaution_einbehalt_step4: If the reason is Nebenkosten, add the latest Betriebskostenabrechnung for comparison.
cat_wohnen_kaution_einbehalt_step5: Do not confuse it with a positive refund.
cat_wohnen_kaution_einbehalt_step6: Classify it as medium to high sensitivity.
cat_wohnen_kaution_rueckzahlung_summary: This is a relatively positive letter since it concerns the return or transfer of the deposit after the tenancy ends. In practical templates it may state that the amount will be transferred automatically to the existing account if no different details are provided, or it may request IBAN, Kontoinhaber, and a new address to complete the Rückzahlung.
cat_wohnen_kaution_rueckzahlung_step1: Read whether the refund is automatic or requires new bank account details.
cat_wohnen_kaution_rueckzahlung_step2: Verify the name and bank account details if they are requested.
cat_wohnen_kaution_rueckzahlung_step3: Understand whether the full amount is being refunded or only the transfer process is being initiated.
cat_wohnen_kaution_rueckzahlung_step4: If a signature or form is required, send it promptly.
cat_wohnen_kaution_rueckzahlung_step5: Monitor the bank account after submitting the details.
cat_wohnen_kaution_rueckzahlung_step6: Keep this letter together with the Kautionsabrechnung if one exists.
cat_rechnung_abschlagsrechnung_summary: This is a partial invoice for an ongoing service or project. It does not indicate a dispute; it means that an agreed instalment is now due, with the final invoice to follow.
cat_rechnung_abschlagsrechnung_step1: Confirm that the invoice relates to an instalment and not the final invoice.
cat_rechnung_abschlagsrechnung_step2: Compare the instalment amount with the original contract or quote.
cat_rechnung_abschlagsrechnung_step3: Check whether the due date for this partial amount is clearly stated.
cat_rechnung_abschlagsrechnung_step4: Pay only the amount requested now, not the full expected total.
cat_rechnung_abschlagsrechnung_step5: Quote the invoice number when making the transfer.
cat_rechnung_abschlagsrechnung_step6: Keep this invoice as it will be offset against the final bill.
cat_rechnung_teilrechnung_summary: This invoice covers a completed stage of work or service. Unlike a deposit invoice, it is linked to work that has actually been carried out, not just an advance payment.
cat_rechnung_teilrechnung_step1: Verify that the stated stage of service has actually been completed.
cat_rechnung_teilrechnung_step2: Review the line items and quantities relating to this phase only.
cat_rechnung_teilrechnung_step3: Do not confuse this with the final Schlussrechnung.
cat_rechnung_teilrechnung_step5: Pay if the items are correct and understandable.
cat_rechnung_teilrechnung_step6: Keep it as it may later be deducted from the overall invoice.
cat_rechnung_schlussrechnung_summary: This is the final overall invoice after completion of the service. It shows the total amount, offsets payments already made, and displays the remaining balance.
cat_rechnung_schlussrechnung_step1: Confirm that all previous advance payments have been deducted.
cat_rechnung_schlussrechnung_step3: Compare the total with the original quote or contract.
cat_rechnung_schlussrechnung_step4: Extract the final payment due date.
cat_rechnung_schlussrechnung_step5: Pay within the deadline if everything is correct.
cat_rechnung_schlussrechnung_step6: Keep this invoice as the final closure of the financial obligation.
cat_rechnung_rechnung_summary: This is a standard invoice for a service rendered or product delivered. It is not a demand letter but the normal start of a payment obligation.
cat_rechnung_rechnung_step2: Confirm it is addressed to you and that the service or product is correct.
cat_rechnung_rechnung_step4: Pay on time and quote the invoice number.
cat_rechnung_rechnung_step5: Keep proof of payment and a copy of the invoice.
cat_rechnung_rechnung_step6: If you find an error, contact the sender immediately before the payment falls due.
cat_rechnung_zahlungserinnerung_summary: This is a friendly payment reminder before a formal demand notice. The tone is still moderate, suggesting the delay may be an oversight. If you have already paid, no further action is needed.
cat_rechnung_zahlungserinnerung_step1: Check immediately whether you have already paid.
cat_rechnung_zahlungserinnerung_step2: If you have paid, review your bank account or transfer receipt.
cat_rechnung_zahlungserinnerung_step3: If not, pay within the stated deadline.
cat_rechnung_zahlungserinnerung_step4: If you do not recognise the debt, locate the original invoice.
cat_rechnung_zahlungserinnerung_step5: Keep proof of payment or correspondence.
cat_rechnung_zahlungserinnerung_step6: Do not delay as the next step is a more formal demand notice.
cat_rechnung_mahnung_summary: This is a formal payment demand. The tone is more direct than a reminder – it explicitly states that you are in arrears and sets a clear deadline.
cat_rechnung_mahnung_step1: Treat this letter with higher priority than a simple reminder.
cat_rechnung_mahnung_step2: Immediately review the original invoice amount and outstanding balance.
cat_rechnung_mahnung_step3: If the claim is valid, pay promptly.
cat_rechnung_mahnung_step4: If there is an error, object in writing or make contact.
cat_rechnung_mahnung_step6: Do not let the deadline pass as additional costs or escalation may follow.
cat_rechnung_letzte_mahnung_summary: This is the final demand before the claim is handed over to a debt collection agency or court. It is the last out-of-court payment request.
cat_rechnung_letzte_mahnung_step1: Treat this letter as the last chance before external escalation.
cat_rechnung_letzte_mahnung_step2: Pay immediately if the claim is valid.
cat_rechnung_letzte_mahnung_step3: If you cannot pay, attempt a direct arrangement before the file is transferred.
cat_rechnung_letzte_mahnung_step4: Pay particular attention to the words 'spätestens' and 'Fristablauf'.
cat_rechnung_letzte_mahnung_step5: Do not wait for another letter from the same sender.
cat_rechnung_letzte_mahnung_step6: Keep a copy of the letter and any evidence of a response or payment.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_forderung_summary: This claim comes from a debt collection agency acting on behalf of the creditor. The total claim includes not only the principal debt but also collection fees and interest, all of which should be critically reviewed.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_forderung_step1: Verify that the claim actually concerns you.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_forderung_step2: Separate the principal debt, collection fees, and interest.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_forderung_step3: If the claim is unjustified, object in writing quickly.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_forderung_step4: If it is valid, do not ignore the letter.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_forderung_step5: Keep all documents and reference numbers.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_forderung_step6: Review the fees critically – not all are automatically lawful.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_ratenzahlung_summar: This is an instalment payment offer from a debt collection company. Such agreements may include additional costs such as settlement fees – do not sign before you have fully understood the terms.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_ratenzahlung_step1: Check the monthly instalment amount and number of instalments carefully.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_ratenzahlung_step2: Look out for possible settlement fees or additional interest.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_ratenzahlung_step3: Do not sign hastily – read the agreement in full.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_ratenzahlung_step4: Only agree to an instalment you can realistically afford.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_ratenzahlung_step5: If you agree, keep a signed and clear copy.
cat_rechnung_inkasso_ratenzahlung_step6: Meet the payment dates – missing them may restart the escalation.
cat_rechnung_mahnbescheid_summary: This is a court document issued in the formal payment order procedure. After service, the recipient generally has two weeks to file an objection or pay. If neither happens, an enforcement order can be applied for.
cat_rechnung_mahnbescheid_step1: Treat this as a court document, not merely a demand letter.
cat_rechnung_mahnbescheid_step2: Calculate the deadline from the actual date of service.
cat_rechnung_mahnbescheid_step3: If the claim is unjustified, file an objection within the deadline.
cat_rechnung_mahnbescheid_step4: If the claim is valid, do not leave it unanswered.
cat_rechnung_mahnbescheid_step5: Keep the envelope and any service documentation.
cat_rechnung_mahnbescheid_step6: Seek legal advice if you are uncertain about your legal position.
cat_rechnung_vollstreckungsbescheid_summ: This is an advanced court stage. After service of the enforcement order, typically two weeks remain to file an objection. After that, compulsory enforcement can proceed.
cat_rechnung_vollstreckungsbescheid_step: Treat this as a greater emergency than a Mahnbescheid.
Immediately check whether an objection (Einspruch) is possible.
Calculate the two-week deadline from the date of service precisely.
Do not ignore it – enforcement may become possible very quickly.
Gather all invoices, letters, and evidence.
Seek legal advice if the claim is disputed or unclear.
cat_rechnung_gerichtsvollzieher_summary: This letter is linked to actual enforcement action by the bailiff. This is no longer just a claim or a debt collection matter but an active enforcement measure based on an existing legal title.
cat_rechnung_gerichtsvollzieher_step1: Do not ignore this letter under any circumstances.
cat_rechnung_gerichtsvollzieher_step2: Note the stated appointment immediately if one is given.
cat_rechnung_gerichtsvollzieher_step3: Clarify which court order the enforcement is based on.
cat_rechnung_gerichtsvollzieher_step4: Gather all documents relating to the debt and the proceedings.
cat_rechnung_gerichtsvollzieher_step5: Seek help or legal advice quickly.
cat_rechnung_gerichtsvollzieher_step6: Treat this as an active enforcement phase, not merely a warning.
cat_rechnung_pfaendung_summary: This is a very advanced compulsory enforcement measure. This is no longer about a demand or debt collection but an actual court-ordered garnishment – usually of a bank account, salary, or claim.
cat_rechnung_pfaendung_step1: Treat this as the most urgent emergency.
cat_rechnung_pfaendung_step2: Seek legal advice or debt counselling immediately.
cat_rechnung_pfaendung_step3: Clarify exactly what has been garnished: bank account, salary, or claim.
cat_rechnung_pfaendung_step4: Do not ignore any further service documents or orders.
cat_rechnung_pfaendung_step5: Gather all documents related to the debt and the enforcement proceedings.
cat_rechnung_pfaendung_step6: Act quickly to organise your financial and legal situation.
cat_bank_sicherheitswarnung_summary: This message means your bank has detected unusual or suspicious activity related to your account or online banking access. This type of message is common in the context of bank security alerts, phishing warnings, and verification of unusual activities.
cat_bank_sicherheitswarnung_step1: Open your bank app or official website directly — never through any link in the message.
cat_bank_sicherheitswarnung_step2: Check your recent transactions and latest login attempts if available.
cat_bank_sicherheitswarnung_step3: If you notice anything unfamiliar, contact your bank immediately.
cat_bank_sicherheitswarnung_step4: Change your password and login credentials if you have any real doubt.
cat_bank_sicherheitswarnung_step5: Scan your device if you clicked any link or downloaded a file.
cat_bank_sicherheitswarnung_step6: Keep the message and its timestamp as a reference in case you need to file a report.
cat_bank_kartensperrung_summary: This message concerns a card freeze or suspected misuse of your credit or debit card. If you did not initiate the block yourself, check your recent card transactions and contact your bank immediately. In an emergency, you can use the German card emergency number 116 116.
cat_bank_kartensperrung_step1: Check whether the card is actually blocked or simply not working.
cat_bank_kartensperrung_step2: Review your recent card transactions in detail.
cat_bank_kartensperrung_step3: If you spot an unfamiliar transaction, report it to your bank immediately.
cat_bank_kartensperrung_step4: Request a replacement card (Ersatzkarte) if the bank confirms it is needed.
cat_bank_kartensperrung_step5: Note the exact time of any suspicious transactions.
cat_bank_kartensperrung_step6: Never click links in the message — contact your bank only through official channels.
cat_bank_sepa_lastschrift_summary: This message means an amount was debited from your account via SEPA direct debit. This is linked to a Mandatsreferenz and Gläubiger-Identifikationsnummer. You have the right to dispute an authorised direct debit within 8 weeks, and up to 13 months if no mandate exists.
cat_bank_sepa_lastschrift_step1: Read the name of the creditor carefully.
cat_bank_sepa_lastschrift_step2: Match the debit against any SEPA mandate you previously authorised.
cat_bank_sepa_lastschrift_step3: Check the amount and the stated reason for the debit.
cat_bank_sepa_lastschrift_step4: If the debit is unfamiliar, object immediately through your bank.
cat_bank_sepa_lastschrift_step5: Monitor your account for similar debits going forward.
cat_bank_sepa_lastschrift_step6: Keep the Mandatsreferenz and Gläubiger-ID details if they appear in the document.
cat_bank_ruecklastschrift_summary: This message means a direct debit could not be executed. The most common reason is insufficient funds. Your bank may charge a fee for the failed debit, and you may need to contact the creditor to arrange payment.
cat_bank_ruecklastschrift_step1: Check the exact reason for the failure immediately.
cat_bank_ruecklastschrift_step2: If the issue is insufficient funds, top up your account quickly.
cat_bank_ruecklastschrift_step3: Verify that the original direct debit was legitimate and authorised.
cat_bank_ruecklastschrift_step4: Contact the creditor so they do not treat you as a late payer.
cat_bank_ruecklastschrift_step5: Watch for any additional fees from your bank or the creditor.
cat_bank_ruecklastschrift_step6: Monitor your account in the following days for a retry or new arrangement.
cat_bank_kontouberziehung_summary: This message means your account has gone into a negative balance or you have exceeded your overdraft limit (Dispositionskredit). There is a difference between the agreed Dispo limit and a tolerated overdraft, and both carry interest costs that can accumulate quickly.
cat_bank_kontouberziehung_step1: Check the exact size of the negative balance.
cat_bank_kontouberziehung_step2: Determine whether the issue is within your Dispo limit or exceeds it.
cat_bank_kontouberziehung_step3: Deposit funds to reduce the negative balance as quickly as possible.
cat_bank_kontouberziehung_step4: Monitor upcoming transactions to prevent the deficit from growing.
cat_bank_kontouberziehung_step5: Check for any overdraft interest or fees being charged.
cat_bank_kontouberziehung_step6: If this happens repeatedly, review your payment patterns or speak to your bank.
cat_bank_kontoauszug_summary: This is a bank statement showing your account transactions and balance. You should review all entries regularly to spot unfamiliar charges or errors and keep the statement as a reference.
cat_bank_kontoauszug_step1: Review every transaction line by line.
cat_bank_kontoauszug_step3: Flag any debit or withdrawal you do not recognise.
cat_bank_kontoauszug_step4: Compare Buchungstag and Wertstellung where relevant.
cat_bank_kontoauszug_step5: Save or download the statement from your online postbox.
cat_bank_kontoauszug_step6: If you spot an unusual entry, contact your bank promptly.
cat_bank_kreditkartenabrechnung_summary: This is a credit card statement listing your card transactions for a specific billing period and the total amount that will be charged. It is separate from a regular bank statement and focuses on card spending.
cat_bank_kreditkartenabrechnung_step1: Review all card transactions for the stated billing period.
cat_bank_kreditkartenabrechnung_step3: Report any transaction you do not recognise immediately.
cat_bank_kreditkartenabrechnung_step4: Do not confuse this statement with your regular Kontoauszug.
cat_bank_kreditkartenabrechnung_step5: Keep the statement as a reference for disputes.
cat_bank_kreditkartenabrechnung_step6: If you find a suspicious entry, request an investigation or card block if necessary.
cat_bank_ueberweisungsbestaetigung_summa: This document confirms that a bank transfer has been successfully executed. It serves as proof of payment and contains key details such as the recipient, IBAN, amount, and reference.
cat_bank_ueberweisungsbestaetigung_step4: Use it as proof if the other party claims payment was not received.
cat_bank_ueberweisungsbestaetigung_step5: Compare it against your Kontoauszug later.
cat_bank_ueberweisungsbestaetigung_step6: Archive it if the transfer is important.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_eingerichtet_summa: This message confirms that a standing order (Dauerauftrag) has been successfully created. A standing order is a recurring automatic transfer executed at a fixed interval to the same recipient.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_eingerichtet_step3: Confirm the first execution date and the payment rhythm.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_eingerichtet_step4: Make sure you were the one who set it up.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_eingerichtet_step5: Correct any error before the first execution date.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_eingerichtet_step6: Monitor the first execution in your Kontoauszug.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_geaendert_summary: This message means a standing order has been modified or deleted. The change or deletion date is important to avoid missing critical recurring payments.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_geaendert_step1: Check exactly what changed: amount, date, or full cancellation.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_geaendert_step2: Confirm the change was intentional and not an error.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_geaendert_step3: Check the next execution date if the order still exists.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_geaendert_step4: If it involves an important payment, correct any mistake immediately.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_geaendert_step5: Keep this message as a reference.
cat_bank_dauerauftrag_geaendert_step6: Monitor your Kontoauszug to confirm the new behaviour.
cat_bank_bankgebuehren_summary: This message or transaction relates to bank fees charged to your account. Not all bank fees are legally permitted, so you should check the type and amount of the charge and verify it against your bank's current fee schedule.
cat_bank_bankgebuehren_step1: Check the exact amount of the fee.
cat_bank_bankgebuehren_step2: Identify the type of fee: account maintenance, replacement card, or other.
cat_bank_bankgebuehren_step3: Verify it matches your bank's current terms and conditions.
cat_bank_bankgebuehren_step4: Compare the entry against your Kontoauszug or Preis- und Leistungsverzeichnis.
cat_bank_bankgebuehren_step5: If the fee seems unjustified, contact your bank in writing.
cat_bank_bankgebuehren_step6: Keep a record of fees if you plan to dispute or compare them later.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_summary: This document is the cancellation notice itself, meaning the contract holder or customer is submitting the cancellation — not that the company has confirmed it yet. The most important linguistic feature is direct phrasing such as 'hiermit kündige ich' or 'ich kündige den Vertrag'. This is critical because you later need proof that the cancellation was actually sent and on what date. In many consumer contracts in Germany, the method and timing of delivery matter greatly, especially where there is a Kündigungsfrist, Kündigungsbutton, or Textform requirement.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_step1: Confirm the document actually contains the cancellation act itself, not just a company confirmation.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_step2: Save the date of sending or submission immediately.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_step3: Keep any proof of delivery: email, fax report, registered letter, or electronic confirmation.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_step4: Check whether you requested cancellation at the earliest possible date or on a specific date.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_step5: Wait for a Kündigungsbestätigung later and do not assume the cancellation is final without it.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_step6: If no response arrives, follow up with the company before the deadline expires.
cat_vertrag_kuendigungsbestaetigung_summ: This letter confirms that the cancellation was received and registered or accepted. It is one of the most important contract letters because its evidentiary value is very high: in the event of a later dispute, this letter is the strongest proof that the company received the cancellation and set the contract end date. In some sectors such as energy, confirmations must include clear information about the contract end, which is why this category must remain separate from a simple contract end notice.
cat_vertrag_kuendigungsbestaetigung_step: Save the letter immediately as official proof.
Confirm the contract end date stated.
Compare the date with the date you requested in your cancellation.
Verify that the contract or customer number is correct.
Keep the letter together with the original cancellation request in the same place.
If the date differs or seems wrong, object directly and in writing.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_anbieter_summary: This letter means the company itself has decided to terminate the contract. This is a fundamental difference from a customer-initiated cancellation, because here the acting party is the provider, not the customer. In telecommunications and other sectors, similar letters can also appear when the provider changes terms or exercises its own right of termination, so phrasing such as 'wir kündigen' and 'wir beenden' must be captured clearly.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_anbieter_step1: Read the reason for cancellation carefully and do not rely only on the letter heading.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_anbieter_step2: Save the date the service stops or the contract ends.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_anbieter_step3: If the service is important, search for an alternative quickly.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_anbieter_step4: If the reason is unclear, request a written explanation from the company.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_anbieter_step5: Keep the full letter, especially if it contains any hint of a right to object or a deadline.
cat_vertrag_kuendigung_anbieter_step6: Watch for any remaining obligation such as returning a device or a final invoice.
cat_vertrag_automatische_verlaengerung_s: This letter means the contract will continue automatically or has been automatically extended if not cancelled within the deadline. This is very important in Germany because modern consumer contract rules have restricted the ability to bind a consumer to long extensions after the initial period, but exceptions exist and some sectors such as insurance follow different logic. This category must clearly capture phrases such as 'verlängert sich automatisch', Mindestlaufzeit, and Kündigungsfrist.
Check the new duration for which the contract will continue.
Review the cancellation notice period carefully.
Do not assume the contract will end on its own without action.
If you do not wish to continue, submit a cancellation immediately.
Keep the letter because it contains an important date for the deadline or start of the extension.
If the contract is old, note that renewal rules may differ from newer contracts.
cat_vertrag_vertragsende_summary: This letter focuses on the fact that the contract ends on a specific date. The reason may be a prior cancellation, natural expiry of the term, or discontinuation of the contractual relationship. The key difference from a cancellation confirmation is that the focus here is on the end point itself rather than confirming receipt of a cancellation. In energy and telecoms, knowing the exact end date is very important to avoid service interruption or an unwanted extension.
cat_vertrag_vertragsende_step1: Save the contract end date immediately.
cat_vertrag_vertragsende_step2: Decide whether you need an alternative before this date.
cat_vertrag_vertragsende_step3: Check whether any action is required before the end, such as returning a device or settling a balance.
cat_vertrag_vertragsende_step4: Do not confuse this letter with a cancellation confirmation.
cat_vertrag_vertragsende_step5: Monitor whether the service will stop automatically or requires new activation.
cat_vertrag_vertragsende_step6: Keep the letter with the rest of the contract documents.
cat_vertrag_arbeitsvertrag_summary: This is an employment contract defining the relationship between employee and employer. In Germany, key elements include the nature of the role, salary, working hours, leave, and notice periods. The probationary period, working hours, and annual leave are elements the employee needs to understand from the outset.
cat_vertrag_arbeitsvertrag_step1: Read the gross salary or salary calculation method carefully.
cat_vertrag_arbeitsvertrag_step2: Verify the number of weekly working hours.
cat_vertrag_arbeitsvertrag_step3: Review the probationary period and the conditions for terminating the contract during it.
cat_vertrag_arbeitsvertrag_step4: Confirm the number of annual leave days.
cat_vertrag_arbeitsvertrag_step5: Read the place of work and the nature of the role or duties.
cat_vertrag_arbeitsvertrag_step6: Do not sign before understanding every key term, especially the notice period and salary.
cat_vertrag_stromvertrag_summary: This is an electricity supply contract and you need to understand elements such as the unit price per kilowatt-hour, the base price, contract duration, and the cancellation notice period. Modern consumer contracts may not bind the customer to long extensions after the initial period, though offers such as bonuses or price guarantees require careful reading.
cat_vertrag_stromvertrag_step1: Review the unit price per kilowatt-hour.
cat_vertrag_stromvertrag_step2: Check the monthly or annual base price.
cat_vertrag_stromvertrag_step3: Pay attention to the contract duration and minimum term.
cat_vertrag_stromvertrag_step4: Understand the cancellation notice period precisely.
cat_vertrag_stromvertrag_step5: Read whether there is a bonus or price guarantee and what its conditions are.
cat_vertrag_stromvertrag_step6: Do not agree before comparing prices with another offer if the contract is new.
cat_vertrag_internetvertrag_summary: This is an internet or mobile phone contract, typically structured around the monthly price, minimum term, speed or data volume, and additional fees such as a connection charge. Telecommunications contracts have specific rules around minimum terms, automatic extension, and the right to cancel after extension with one month's notice, as well as matters like number portability and provider switching.
cat_vertrag_internetvertrag_step3: Understand the current cancellation notice period and after the extension.
cat_vertrag_internetvertrag_step4: Confirm the actual speed or data volume.
cat_vertrag_internetvertrag_step5: Watch for fees such as a connection charge or devices tied to the contract.
cat_vertrag_internetvertrag_step6: If switching providers, plan early to avoid a service gap.
cat_vertrag_versicherungsvertrag_summary: This is an insurance contract or the foundational document establishing the insurance relationship. Key information such as the policy document, general insurance conditions, the start of cover, and the scope of protection are central to understanding the contract. Many insurance contracts renew automatically if not cancelled in time, so this category must be clearly separated from premium invoices, claims notices, and policy conditions found in the Insurance folder.
cat_vertrag_versicherungsvertrag_step1: Identify the type of insurance clearly first.
cat_vertrag_versicherungsvertrag_step2: Read the start of cover and when protection actually begins.
cat_vertrag_versicherungsvertrag_step3: Review the scope of coverage and what the contract actually covers.
cat_vertrag_versicherungsvertrag_step4: Understand the premium amount and payment method.
cat_vertrag_versicherungsvertrag_step5: Read the general insurance conditions at least for the key points.
cat_vertrag_versicherungsvertrag_step6: Do not confuse this contract with a premium invoice, claims notice, or cancellation.
cat_vertrag_kaufvertrag_summary: This is a purchase contract between a seller and a buyer. The most distinguishing elements are the purchase price, the item being sold, both parties' details, and the delivery or handover conditions or defects. The question of defects and warranty is central, especially in private purchases or where warranty exclusion language appears.
cat_vertrag_kaufvertrag_step2: Identify the item being sold and its full description.
cat_vertrag_kaufvertrag_step3: Review any defects mentioned in the contract.
cat_vertrag_kaufvertrag_step4: Pay attention to the delivery or handover date.
cat_vertrag_kaufvertrag_step5: Understand whether a warranty applies or has been excluded.
cat_vertrag_kaufvertrag_step6: Do not sign if the description, defects, or price are unclear.
cat_vertrag_ratenzahlungsvertrag_summary: This is an instalment payment or consumer financing contract. The most important comparison metric is not the small monthly instalment but the effective annual interest rate, and such contracts typically include duration, total amount, number of instalments, and core credit data. What often appears to be a zero-percent financing offer is in reality an organised credit contract with a financing partner.
cat_vertrag_ratenzahlungsvertrag_step2: Understand the effective annual interest rate properly.
cat_vertrag_ratenzahlungsvertrag_step3: Calculate the total amount you will ultimately pay.
cat_vertrag_ratenzahlungsvertrag_step4: Review the number of instalments and the contract duration.
cat_vertrag_ratenzahlungsvertrag_step5: Watch for any additional costs not visible in the instalment itself.
cat_vertrag_ratenzahlungsvertrag_step6: Do not agree if the final total is far higher than the value of the financed item.
cat_vertrag_mitgliedschaft_summary: This is a membership or subscription contract, such as a gym, platform, newspaper, or recurring service. The two most dangerous points are typically the minimum term and automatic renewal. Modern contracts may not lock the consumer into a long new term after the initial period and must remain terminable on short notice, but older contracts may differ.
cat_vertrag_mitgliedschaft_step3: Understand the current cancellation notice period.
cat_vertrag_mitgliedschaft_step4: Note whether there is automatic renewal.
cat_vertrag_mitgliedschaft_step5: Keep the membership start date and expected end date.
cat_vertrag_mitgliedschaft_step6: If you no longer need the subscription, do not wait until the last moment.
